generation.method: exact scheduler npm tarball plus local scheduler implementation copied under an isolated alias into a temporary node_modules tree and probed through isolated Node child processes with controlled Task Scheduling API shims
generation.probeIsolation: one Node child process per target, scenario, and mode
generation.timingNormalization: raw wall-clock timestamps are omitted; probes use a controlled window.performance.now shim and record only logical scheduler calls, priority values, delay value categories, abort state, and shouldYield booleans; local package metadata and alias subpath resolution are omitted from behavior comparison

afterWorker125.source: packages/scheduler/unstable_post_task.js and cjs/scheduler-unstable_post_task.* are copied under fast-react-scheduler for behavior comparison

probeModes.reason: The scheduler/unstable_post_task wrapper selects cjs/scheduler-unstable_post_task.development.js when NODE_ENV is not production.
The scheduler/unstable_post_task wrapper selects cjs/scheduler-unstable_post_task.production.js when NODE_ENV is production.

captures: load success with minimal window globals
schedule failure when TaskController is absent
schedule failure when global scheduler is absent
schedule failure when scheduler.postTask is absent

scenarios.area: post-task scheduling calls under a controlled Task Scheduling API shim
captures: public priority to postTask priority mapping
returned task node and controller descriptor shape
deadline-based shouldYield behavior with controlled time
aborted task is skipped by the controlled shim
continuation falls back to scheduler.postTask when scheduler.yield is absent

packageJson.name: scheduler
packageJson.description: Cooperative scheduler for the browser environment.

fastReactScheduler.source: local packages/scheduler copied for behavior comparison

implementationRisks: The local scheduler package metadata intentionally remains workspace-specific and is excluded from scheduler/unstable_post_task behavior comparisons.

The oracle intentionally covers the post-task scheduler variant only; root scheduler, mock scheduler, native scheduler, reconciler lane scheduling, and renderer behavior remain separate compatibility surfaces.
The Task Scheduling API is represented by a controlled Node shim; the comparison does not claim real browser host task ordering or raw timing compatibility.
timingCaveats: The checked artifact intentionally omits raw times and absolute timestamps.
The Task Scheduling API is represented by a controlled Node shim; it records scheduler module calls but does not claim browser host task ordering.
The controlled shim uses deterministic window.performance.now values to classify shouldYield behavior around the module's 5ms local deadline.
Continuation probes assert whether scheduler.yield or scheduler.postTask is called, not browser promise scheduling latency.
